title: Recursive Containers, Stack Parsing, Depth, and Recovery · Language Clinic: Nesting Evidence
summary: Deeply nested lists overflow recursion, an unmatched container consumes the rest of the file, child offsets drift, and one invalid construct prevents later valid content from rendering. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the nesting evidence increment in bounded nested-block parser and recovery trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.
Emit a located diagnostic, preserve literal source where safe, resynchronize at a documented boundary, and continue independent parsing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-nesting-recursion-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-nesting-recursion-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_nesting_recursion_3(containers_opened: int = 7, containers_closed: int = 7, max_depth: int = 6, recovery_points: int = 2, stalled_positions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if containers_opened < 0 or containers_closed < 0:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-count"
    if containers_closed != containers_opened:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-balance"
    if max_depth < 0 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    if recovery_points < 1 or stalled_positions > 0:
        return False, stalled_positions, "recovery-progress"
    assert containers_closed == containers_opened
    return True, max_depth, "recoverable-nesting"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-nesting-evidence-7t1lwi
# Competency: ssg-nesting-evidence.
# Case 1679: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_nesting_evidence_7t1lwi(containers_opened: int = 7, containers_closed: int = 7, max_depth: int = 6, recovery_points: int = 2, stalled_positions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_7t1lwi = "7t1lwi-iwl1t7-1679"
    assert evidence_variant_7t1lwi
    evidence_axes_7t1lwi = (
        "reader7t1lwi",
        "source1679",
        "parseiwl1t7",
        "route7t1lwi1679",
        "artifact16797t1lwi",
        "recoveryiwl1t71679",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_7t1lwi)) == 6
    if containers_opened < 0 or containers_closed < 0:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-count"
    if containers_closed != containers_opened:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-balance"
    if max_depth < 0 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    if recovery_points < 1 or stalled_positions > 0:
        return False, stalled_positions, "recovery-progress"
    assert containers_closed == containers_opened
    return True, max_depth, "recoverable-nesting"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Nesting Evidence model · Theory Nesting Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Nesting Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 9508 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound recursive containers, guarantee source advancement, and retain recovery evidence; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Nesting Evidence model · Theory Nesting Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Nesting Evidence model · Theory Nesting Evidence, keep Nesting Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Nesting Evidence model · Theory Nesting Evidence, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Nesting Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Nesting Evidence model · Theory Nesting Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 9508 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound recursive containers, guarantee source advancement, and retain recovery evidence; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Nesting Evidence model · Theory Nesting Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: One deeply nested happy path covers container interactions.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-nesting-evidence-7t1lwi.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-nesting-evidence-7t1lwi(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_nesting_evidence_7t1lwi\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?containers_opened < 0 or containers_closed < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?containers_closed != containers_opened)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?max_depth < 0 or max_depth > 64)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?recovery_points < 1 or stalled_positions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
